title: hieracium sylvaticum subsp. caespitans
intro: Hieracium sylvaticum subsp. caespitans, also known as the Wood Hawkweed or Alpine Hawkweed, is a rare and endangered subspecies of the Hieracium sylvaticum plant. This perennial herb is characterized by its yellow flowers and slender stems that grow in alpine and subalpine regions. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, Hieracium sylvaticum subsp. caespitans is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can help to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and provide antioxidant benefits.
